result:
Performing C-LDIP-04 read-only adjudication review; no files will be modified. Findings below.

```json
{
  "review_id": "C-LDIP-04",
  "model": "claude-opus-4-8",
  "mode": "read-only",
  "effort": "max",
  "overall_verdict": "PASS_WITH_FINDINGS",
  "blocks_ldip_planning_gate": false,
  "blocks_ldip_implementation_gate": true,
  "no_unresolved_p0": true,
  "no_unresolved_p1": true,
  "summary": "Gap adjudication is sound and conservative on the safety-critical runtime families (SEC=adapt_required; SRCH/AGT/TOOL/CLEAN/COMP/API/OUT=new_required; audit-first/evidence-bound output/human-review all new/adapt). All four AI-accident rejects (901-904) and the high-leakage defers (PRIO-302 similar-matter, 303 redaction) are justified and routed. The prose implementation gate (gap-adjudication condition 3) and the JSON gate (adjudications_complete=false, ldip_implementation_allowed=false, c_ldip_04_complete=false) all correctly block implementation now — no P0. No high-risk leakage/unsafe-AI/external-share runtime family is wrongly marked covered/weakly_covered — no P1. Remaining issues are classification-consistency, scope-quantification, and enumeration gaps to resolve before the implementation gate opens.",
  "findings": [
    {
      "id": "C-LDIP-04-F1",
      "severity": "P2",
      "title": "Implementation-admission gate is structurally asymmetric: C-LDIP-04 has no c_ldip_04_adjudicated field.",
      "evidence": "ldip-overlay-closeout-pack-map.json -> implementation_admission_gate carries both _complete and _adjudicated for C-LDIP-01/02/03, but C-LDIP-04 carries only c_ldip_04_complete. The aggregate adjudications_complete=false is the sole machine-readable signal that this very review's findings were adjudicated before ldip_implementation_allowed can flip. The prose gate (gap-adjudication 'Implementation Admission Decision' cond. 3) does require C-LDIP-04 adjudicated, so the gate is not currently open.",
      "recommended_action": "Add c_ldip_04_adjudicated:false to the gate and require it (with adjudications_complete) before ldip_implementation_allowed can be true, restoring per-review symmetry and auditability of the final gate flip."
    },
    {
      "id": "C-LDIP-04-F2",
      "severity": "P2",
      "title": "Family/cross-cutting rollups are not the most-conservative member: LDIP-ARCH, Vendor Neutrality, and LDIP-PRIO under-classified as weakly_covered.",
      "evidence": "gap-adjudication Family table: LDIP-ARCH=weakly_covered though requirement-candidates lists LDIP-ARCH-005=adapt_required_before_implementation; LDIP-PRIO=weakly_covered though PRIO-001 (build-order) and PRIO-004 (final recommendation)=adapt_required and PRIO-301-310=defer; cross-cutting 'Vendor Neutrality'=weakly_covered (a named LDIP core invariant, execution-plan 4.5, with product/vendor-lock-in drift risk). Contrast LDIP-SEC and LDIP-RISK, which correctly roll up to adapt_required, proving the rollup can be done conservatively. The binding action text already matches adapt_required, so the substance exists but the label understates scrutiny.",
      "recommended_action": "Raise LDIP-ARCH and the Vendor-Neutrality cross-cutting decision to adapt_required, and LDIP-PRIO to adapt_required (or split it into adapt_required + defer rows); add an explicit rule to the Decision Taxonomy that a family decision equals its most-conservative member."
    },
    {
      "id": "C-LDIP-04-F3",
      "severity": "P2",
      "title": "new_required scope volume is unquantified; expanded_total_units accounts for HRX only, not LDIP — risk of work underestimation / pack under-sizing.",
      "evidence": "overlay live_baseline: current=54355, hrx=901, expanded_total_units=55256 (LDIP=+0). ~16 of 20 families are new_required, yet rp-anchor-map attaches them to existing CP ranges (e.g., LDIP-AGT 12 agents -> CP00-740-785, LDIP-CLEAN room-types/objects/policies -> CP00-825-869) with no unit-count delta. overlay_policy.new_units_require_user_approved_ledger_extension=true and admission cond. 5 gate new units, but the magnitude is never estimated, leaving absorb-into-existing-range vs. extend ambiguous for behavior that is by definition not covered by existing scope.",
      "recommended_action": "Quantify (or bound) the new-unit impact of the new_required families and reconcile against expanded_total_units, OR explicitly state that anchoring means absorption with demonstrated headroom; until then, gate each affected pack on a recorded ledger-extension decision so risk-class sizing cannot silently understate boundary-sensitive LDIP work."
    },
    {
      "id": "C-LDIP-04-F4",
      "severity": "P3",
      "title": "Third-wave defers PRIO-306-310 lack individual justification and revisit gates (no-omission gap).",
      "evidence": "gap-adjudication 'Explicit Rejections And Defers' enumerates PRIO-301..305 only; requirement-candidates classifies the full LDIP-PRIO-301-310 third wave as defer_with_revisit_gate, so 306-310 are deferred at range level without per-item reason or revisit gate, against execution-plan 4.2 'Nothing may be silently omitted.'",
      "recommended_action": "Enumerate PRIO-306..310 individually with reason + revisit gate, or explicitly state they are covered by the 301-305 gates."
    },
    {
      "id": "C-LDIP-04-F5",
      "severity": "P2",
      "title": "Boundary-sensitive Risk-A enumeration omits the foundational ingestion layer (RP01/LDIP-SRC/LDIP-CAT) and mass-data migration (RP25).",
      "evidence": "overlay boundary_sensitive_risk_policy.applies_to_families omits LDIP-SRC and LDIP-CAT; applies_to_rps omits RP01 and RP25. RP01 owns source-connector matter-mapping and owner scope — the first cross-matter/cross-tenant leakage vector — and RP25 performs security-label/DMS/email/VDR backfill (mass boundary movement, mislabel-then-overshare risk). Both rely solely on the applies_when keyword catch-all rather than explicit enumeration alongside RP16/RP20, despite handling unauthorized-data, cross-tenant, masking, and external-sharing surfaces.",
      "recommended_action": "Add LDIP-SRC and LDIP-CAT to the boundary-sensitive families and RP01 and RP25 to the boundary-sensitive RPs, or explicitly document that the applies_when catch-all governs RP01 ingestion/owner-mapping and RP25 label/VDR backfill at Risk A."
    }
  ],
  "review_question_notes": {
    "q1_decisions_conservative_enough": "Mostly yes. Runtime/leakage families are conservative. Exceptions: ARCH/Vendor-Neutrality/PRIO rollups under-conservative vs. their adapt_required members (F2).",
    "q2_rejects_defers_justified_and_routed": "Yes for 901-905 and PRIO-301-305 (clear reasons + revisit gates). Gap: PRIO-306-310 routed only at range level (F4).",
    "q3_blocks_implementation_until_c_ldip_complete": "Yes — prose + JSON gates all block now; only structural defect is the missing c_ldip_04_adjudicated field (F1). No P0.",
    "q4_cross_cutting_gaps_serious_enough": "Matter-first=adapt_required, Permission-intersection=adapt_required, Audit-first=new_required, Evidence-bound-output=new_required are adequate. Vendor neutrality is the weak link (F2).",
    "q5_high_risk_wrongly_covered": "No runtime-leakage family is wrongly lenient. Architecture/vendor-neutrality (drift) is the only borderline-lenient label (F2); ingestion/migration under-emphasized in boundary enumeration (F5)."
  }
}
```

Net: PASS_WITH_FINDINGS — no P0/P1. The adjudication does not let implementation start early (gate correctly blocks), but five fixes (F1, F2, F3, F5 = P2; F4 = P3) should be adjudicated before the implementation gate opens.
